mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no significant negative correlation with FEDFUNDS levels (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals immediate labor market deterioration, triggering sharp risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the trigger condition fails to predict direction with a log-odds ratio > 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in real sales of existing homes and housing starts indicate a synchronized collapse in residential demand, which transmits to broader corporate revenues and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 60-day returns with a statistically significant coefficient (p > 0.05) in a multivariate regression over any 3-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: An index of industrial production exceeding its 12-month moving average confirms robust manufacturing and utility output, which sustains corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO levels above the 12-month moving average coincide with negative 20-day forward returns in > 55% of observations during post-shock recovery periods, or the signal predictive power vanishes outside the specified regime.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation print exceeding 0.3 percent forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of instances when CPIAUCSL rises above 0.3 percent, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions (Mann-Whitney U p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE yields a negative or null 60-day forward return in > 50% of triggered months, or the interaction term is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects stronger aggregate demand and corporate revenue expansion, which directly translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings expectations adjust upward.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no statistically significant positive correlation with 20-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.01) over any 3-year rolling window, or the signal fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month industrial production decline exceeding 0.5 percent signals immediate supply-side contraction and weakening economic activity, triggering risk-off flows and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances when INDPRO falls below the -0.5 percent threshold, or the trigger condition fails to predict direction with a log-odds ratio > 1.5.
